business.headline: Buying a car or bike? The hidden truth behind insurance sold at dealershipsWhen you buy a car or bike, the final bill usually comes as an 'on-road price' that includes insurance. For most buyers, this is accepted without much thought, as it appears to be a standard part of the purchase. But what often goes unnoticed is that this insurance is not mandatory to buy from the dealer.

Middle East war to cut global growth, spike inflation despite truce: World BankThe World Bank's baseline estimate now projects growth in emerging markets and developing economies of 3.65 per cent in 2026, compared to 4 per cent in October, dropping as low as 2.6 per cent in an adverse scenario with a longer-lasting war.

India allows 4 sanctioned tankers carrying Iranian oil to berth at Sikka: ReportIranian oil is often transported by a so-called shadow fleet of vessels that lack internationally recognised insurance and safety certifications. But this requires special permission from the government as exemptions are required under Indian rules for the berthing of ships.

Gold dips, silver slips: Is this the right time to invest or wait?Prices of gold and silver have cooled off after recent highs, but the bigger question remains, is this the right entry point, or is there more downside ahead?

Why April is the best time to reset your budget, SIPs and other investmentsIn Part 2 of our MONEY MAKEOVER series, we explore how April, with salary hikes, bonuses and tax changes, creates the perfect window to pause, reset and move forward with clarity. Let's have a look.

Necessary evolution: SP Group backs Tata Sons listing, seeks RBI clarityIn a statement, Shapoorji Pallonji Mistry said a timely listing is not merely about regulatory compliance, but a step that would reinforce the foundational principles of the Tata Group.

Global shocks may hit every 1–2 years, warns Axis Bank's chief economistAxis Bank chief economist Neelkanth Mishra warns that geopolitical shocks could hit every 1–2 years and explains what India must do to prepare its economy.

Equity inflows jump 55% in March, but debt outflows drag industry negative: AMFIThe data clearly shows that retail investors are becoming more confident and disciplined. While institutional money continues to move in and out of debt funds based on short-term needs, individual investors are steadily increasing their exposure to equities.
Dalal Street records best week in 5 years on Iran-US ceasefire reliefSensex closes 919 points higher, Nifty crosses 24,000; IT stocks tumble

Unclaimed EPF rises to Rs 10,900 crore: Why it's rising and how to claim your moneyYou may not think much about your old PF account after switching jobs, but it could still be holding your money. Unclaimed EPF funds have now crossed Rs 10,900 crore, raising concerns about how easily savings are getting left behind.

TCS shifts focus to AI, infrastructure as demand outlook remains mixedShares of TCS were trading at Rs 2,513 on the BSE, down 2.89% or Rs 74.75, as of 1:27 pm, even as its fourth-quarter performance beat estimates.

RBI wants a 1-hour pause on some UPI payments. Here's whyThe Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has released a discussion paper proposing a one-hour delay for certain digital payments. Here's an explainer on how it could work, which transactions it may affect, and what it means for users.

Sending over Rs 10,000 via UPI? RBI may soon introduce a 1-hour delayIf you often use UPI for bigger payments, a change may be on the way. The RBI is looking at adding a one-hour pause for transfers above Rs 10,000 to help prevent online fraud.

Applying for TAN? Income Tax Dept replaces Form 49B with two new formsApplying for a TAN is set to become a lot simpler. The Income Tax Department has replaced the old Form 49B with two new forms, aiming to make the process clearer and easier for different types of applicants. Let's have a look.

Infosys, TCS, HCLTech: Why IT stocks are down despite market rallyThe fall in IT stocks comes even as the Sensex and Nifty moved higher, showing that the weakness is limited to the sector amid concerns around growth and demand.

Sun Pharma shares fall over 4% amid acquisition concernsSun Pharma shares fell over 3.5%, as investor concerns over a potential mega acquisition outweighed optimism, bringing focus on debt, integration risks and the deal's long-term payoff.

TCS shares down 3%: Why the IT giant's stock fell despite strong Q4 result?TCS shares down: The stock was trading at around Rs 2,515.00, down 2.81% as of 10:15 am. It was also among the top losers on both the Nifty50 and the IT index.

Wipro shares rise as board considers buyback after three yearsWipro has informed exchanges that its board will evaluate a share buyback alongside its fourth-quarter results on April 16, bringing the focus back on shareholder returns.
Sensex ends 900 points higher: 3 reasons why stock market rose todayThe S&P BSE Sensex climbed 687.99 points to 77,319.64, while the NSE Nifty50 added 211.60 points to 23,986.70 as of 9:58 am.
Ola Electric shares continue to rise, up 6% today. Here's whyAt 9:44 am, Ola Electric shares were trading at Rs 38.71, up Rs 2.37 or 6.52%. Yesterday, the stock closed nearly 20% higher.

entertainment.headline: Legendary singer Asha Bhosle admitted to Mumbai's Breach Candy Hospital Legendary playback singer Asha Bhosle has been admitted to Mumbai's Breach Candy Hospital, after she siffered a cardiac arrest.

Viral: Dhurandhar's Madhavan, Arjun Rampal hope Pak hosts Indian awards in old video An old clip, featuring R Madhavan and Arjun Rampal among other Bollywood stars, shows them hoping Pakistan gets to host an Indian awards show. The video has gone viral amid the success of Dhurandhar: The Revenge.

It's Shoe-time: Watch Meryl Streep, Anne Hathaway, KJo at Devil Wears Prada 2 event Meryl Streep reviewed Karan Johar's footwear during the promotional tour for The Devil Wears Prada 2 in Tokyo. The light-hearted exchange saw Johar foot a query or two from Anne Hathaway, too.

RGV says he never got threats from the underworld: They loved Satya, Company Ram Gopal Varma recalled the underworld's impact on his iconic films, Satya and Company. In turn, he added, the underworld, too, loved his films.

Outcome review: Keanu Reeves shines in black comedy about flaws, self-acceptanceRelease Date:Fri Apr 10, 2026 Outcome review: Jonah Hill's black comedy-drama offers a subdued look at celebrity guilt and human imperfections. The film is elevated by strong performances yet limited by familiar storytelling that doesn't quite linger.

Happy birthday my momo: Saiyaara's Aneet Padda wishes Mohit Suri with a wild video Aneet Padda marked filmmaker Mohit Suri's birthday with a heartfelt Instagram post. The Saiyaara actor also shared a wonky video, adding a dash of humour to her gratitude for the director.

DDLJ in Academy of Motion Pictures list of top travel romance films. Kajol reacts Dilwale Dulhania Le Jayenge has been included in The Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences' list of top romance films that involve travel. Check out how Kajol reacted to the post.

Supriya Ganesh, Maitreya Ramakrishnan slam troll's colourist, 'kamwali bai' jibe Actors Supriya Ganesh and Maitreyi Ramakrishnan hit back at a troll targeting Indian representation in Hollywood. They called out the stereotypes highlighted in a post, also involving Charithra Chandran and Shabana Azeez.

Had a calling: Sara Arjun attends Bhasma Aarti in Ujjain amid Dhurandhar 2 success As Dhurandhar 2 continues to break records at the box office, actor Sara Arjun visited the Mahakaleshwar Temple in Ujjain on Saturday. She attended the Bhasma Aarti and called the experience overwhelming.

Jana Nayagan actor Pooja Hegde hurt by leak: Watch Vijay's last film in theatres Jana Nayagan actor Pooja Hegde reacted to the film's leak by calling it disheartening and a blow to the efforts of the entire team. Urging fans to avoid piracy, she appealed to audiences to watch Vijay's final film the right way.

Ben Affleck gives Jennifer Lopez his share of $60M Beverly Hills home: Report Ben Affleck and Jennifer Lopez's divorce proceedings took a new turn with a fresh update about their Beverly Hills home. The development comes nearly a year after Lopez spoke about the emotional toll of their split.

Jana Nayagan leak: AR Murugadoss, Karthi, Pa Ranjith slam piracy; demand action The leak of Vijay's Jana Nayagan has triggered strong reactions across the South film industry. Actor Karthi and filmmakers AR Murugadoss and Pa Ranjith, among others, have urged audiences to support the film through official platforms.

Can Ranbir Kapoor's Ramayana usher Bollywood's Avatar era with VFX blitz? From Koi... Mil Gaya in 2003 to the upcoming Ramayana, VFX in Bollywood promises to take a next-level leap. With a lavish budget across two films and collaboration with Oscar-winning global VFX giant DNEG, Nitesh Tiwari's directorial could be the boost Hindi cinema needs.

Dhurandhar 2 box office Day 23: Ranveer's film nears Rs 1,700 crore mark globally Dhurandhar: The Revenge is inching closer to the Rs 1,700 crore mark worldwide by continuing its strong box office run into week four. The Ranveer Singh-starrer is now eyeing a spot among the top 3 highest-grossing Indian films of all time.

Real star: Wheelchair cricketer Bhima Khunti hails Ranveer Singh's humility Wheelchair cricketer Bhima Khunti shared his memorable meeting with Ranveer Singh, praising the actor's humility. Here's what he said.

Hai Jawani Toh Ishq Hona Hai may hit theatres sooner than planned: Report Varun Dhawan's Hai Jawani Toh Ishq Hona Hai may release earlier than planned. Here's why the makers are considering a date change.

Systemic failure: Rajinikanth, Kamal Haasan denounce Jana Nayagan leak Systemic failure: Rajinikanth and Kamal Haasan slam the Jana Nayagan leak, highlighting certification delays and the growing threat of piracy.

Nora Fatehi defends 'feminism' remark, says it was taken out of context Nora Fatehi responded to backlash over her feminism remarks, saying her words were taken out of context—here's what she clarified.

Ranveer, Janhvi join Anant Ambani as he cuts birthday cake, jam over bhajan. Videos Actors Ranveer Singh, Janhvi Kapoor and Veer Pahariya were among guests seen jamming over bhajan at Anant Ambani's birthday celebration. A video of the birthday boy cutting cake has also gone viral.

general news.headline: US-Iran talks begin after 5-hour delay, Trump warns of reset if negotiations fail Live UpdatesBefore Donald Trump announced that talks had begun, the US delegation—led by JD Vance and including Jared Kushner—had spent nearly five hours in Islamabad without engaging in any direct discussions with Iranian officials.

US has begun clearing Hormuz: Trump as tensed Iran ceasefire talks underway in PakHe framed the purported move as a favour to countries, including several US allies, that he said did not have the 'courage or will to do this work themselves.'

Why Asim Munir met Iran leaders in combat gear but JD Vance in a suitPakistan army chief Asim Munir sent a carefully choreographed message through his choice of clothes while receiving the Iranian delegation and US Vice President JD Vance as both sides arrived in Islamabad on Saturday for make-or-break peace talks.

If peace talks in Islamabad collapse, the fallout will haunt PakistanThe US and Iranian delegations have begun high-stakes ceasefire talks in Pakistan. However, several geopolitical and economic experts have warned that if the mediation collapses, the fallout could be a nightmare for Pakistan's hybrid military-civilian leadership.

Indian-born cheetah in Kuno welcomes 4 cubs, the 1st recorded birth in the wildA 25-month-old Indian-born female cheetah has given birth to four cubs in Kuno National Park. This marks the first wild birth by a second-generation cheetah since the 2022 reintroduction.
Legendary singer Asha Bhosle admitted to Mumbai's Breach Candy HospitalLegendary playback singer Asha Bhosle has been admitted to Mumbai's Breach Candy Hospital, after she siffered a cardiac arrest.

Mojtaba Khamenei has severe, disfiguring wounds from strikes that killed fatherIran's new Supreme Leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, is still recovering from severe injuries sustained in an airstrike that killed his father, while continuing to participate in key decisions through audio meetings.

This hydrogen stove runs on water, not gas and could replace traditional cylindersHydrogen cooking stove India, green hydrogen stove, no LPG cooking. This Made-in-India device runs on hydrogen instead of gas cylinders and is already being explored for kitchens, labs, and large-scale use.

Donald J Trump, President of Venezuela"The people of Venezuela, they say, if I ran for president of Venezuela, I'm polling higher than anybody has ever polled in Venezuela, so after I'm finished with this, I can go to Venezuela. I will quickly learn Spanish. It won't take too long. I'm good at language and I will go to Venezuela. I'm going to run for president." President Donald J. Trump, April 5, 2026, Washington D.C.

Amity, Ashoka join IITs in shaping the Cognitive Science revolution, driving Artificial IntelligenceCognitive science is, by its very nature, a field that requires the simultaneous presence of psychology, neuroscience, computer science, linguistics and philosophy.

CSK vs DC Live: Samson slams maiden ton for CSK, Mhatre retired outChennai Super Kings (CSK) vs Delhi Capitals (DC) Live Score, IPL 2026: CSK (184/2 in 18 ovs) CSK take full control as Samson nears a century and Mhatre supports, with DC struggling for answers as the scoring surges and a big total looks firmly on the cards.

10 returning home from Jharkhand, die after pickup truck collides with bus in BiharAt least 10 people were killed and 25–30 injured after a pickup vehicle collided head-on with a bus in Bihar's Katihar district on Saturday.

Watch: PM, Rahul greet each other, engage in rare candid chat in ParliamentThe duo had arrived at Prerna Sthal to pay tributes to 19th-century social reformer Jyotiba Phule on his 199th birth anniversary. The sight was a warm departure from the usual interpersonal dynamics publicly witnessed between PM Modi and Rahul Gandhi, which is primarily characterised by the cut-throat jibes they exchange in the political arena.

Trump boasts US has more oil than Russia, Saudi as empty tankers head to its shoresAmid US-Iran ceasefire talks in Pakistan, Donald Trump said he is awaiting empty oil tankers headed to American shores, adding that the US is ready to export the "sweetest oil (and gas!) anywhere in the world."

Viral: Dhurandhar's Madhavan, Arjun Rampal hope Pak hosts Indian awards in old videoAn old clip, featuring R Madhavan and Arjun Rampal among other Bollywood stars, shows them hoping Pakistan gets to host an Indian awards show. The video has gone viral amid the success of Dhurandhar: The Revenge.

Iran firm on Hormuz reopening, Lebanon ceasefire as delayed US talks begin in PakIran has set key preconditions for direct talks with the US, including the release of frozen assets, safeguarding the Strait of Hormuz, reparations, and a regional ceasefire. Amid these demands, the White House has maintained a hardline stance, with Donald Trump refusing to concede, including on the issue of unfreezing overseas assets.

What is a plasma electric stove? The gas-free flame tech explainedA new electric flame plasma stove is grabbing attention for creating real flame-like cooking without LPG. Here's what this technology is, how it works, and whether it could change the way Indian kitchens cook.
Question Time: Weekly News QuizThink you read the headlines this week? Let's test that!

IPL 2026: PBKS gun down 220 to crush SRH despite Abhishek Sharma's heroicsPunjab Kings outclassed Sunrisers Hyderabad in a battle of explosive top orders, chasing down 220 with authority in Mullanpur to seal a six-wicket win in IPL 2026 and spoil what had begun as Abhishek Sharma's night of dominance.

War to escalate? China preparing to secretly send arms to Iran, says US intelWith the ceasefire on shaky ground and Trump sending more troops to the Middle East, there are ample signs of the conflict escalating in the coming weeks. Against this backdrop, US intel suggests China may ship new air defence systems to Iran.

Can Ranbir Kapoor's Ramayana usher Bollywood's Avatar era with VFX blitz?From Koi... Mil Gaya in 2003 to the upcoming Ramayana, VFX in Bollywood promises to take a next-level leap. With a lavish budget across two films and collaboration with Oscar-winning global VFX giant DNEG, Nitesh Tiwari's directorial could be the boost Hindi cinema needs.

Indian techie requests flexible hours to care for newborn, US manager's reply is viralAn Indian techie's honest message about wanting flexible working hours to care for his newborn baby was met with warmth by his American manager, emphasising trust and a healthy work culture.

China helping Pak replace AWACS aircraft India shot down in Op Sindoor?Pakistan is likely to acquire Shaanxi KJ-500, an Airborne Warning and Control System (AWACS) aircraft, from China. Negotiations started in June 2025. Just a month before that, India had shot down at least one AWACS aircraft of Pakistan in Operation Sindoor. AWACS is considered a major asset and force multiplier.

Video: Pak F-16 jets escort JD Vance to Islamabad for ceasefire talks with IranUS Vice President JD Vance is scheduled to meet an Iranian delegation later today, marking the highest-level direct talks between Washington and Tehran since the 1979 Islamic Revolution. Ahead of the high-stakes ceasefire talks, the US delegation landed in Islamabad after being escorted by Pakistani F-16 fighter jets.

sports.headline: Sanju Samson makes mass comeback at Chepauk, slams maiden IPL hundred for CSK Sanju Samson has made a sensational comeback to form as he scored his maiden IPL hundred for Chennai Super Kings during the clash against Delhi Capitals on Saturday, April 11.

Ishan Kishan laments SRH's poor finish with the bat in six-wicket loss to PBKS Ishan Kishan admitted that Sunrisers Hyderabad fell well short of a potential 250 total despite a flying start, pointing to poor execution with the bat in the death overs. Punjab Kings, led by Shreyas Iyer, capitalised on that lapse with a composed chase to seal a six-wicket win.

Advantage Manchester City? Arsenal stumble in title race with Bournemouth loss Alex Scott's decisive strike halts Arsenal's momentum and tightens the title race as the Gunners lose 1-2 to Bournemouth at the Emirates Stadium. This means that the advantage is with Manchester City, who have two games in hand over Arsenal.

Animesh Kujur overcomes false start setback, shines with 100m-200m double Star sprinter Animesh Kujur of Odisha dominated the Indian Athletics Series one-day meet, winning both the 100m and 200m events in a competitive outing on Saturday.

IPL 2026: PBKS gun down 220 to crush SRH despite Abhishek Sharma's heroics Punjab Kings outclassed Sunrisers Hyderabad in a battle of explosive top orders, chasing down 220 with authority in Mullanpur to seal a six-wicket win in IPL 2026 and spoil what had begun as Abhishek Sharma's night of dominance.

Auqib Nabi finally makes IPL debut for DC, Dewald Brevis returns for CSK Auqib Nabi finally gets the chance to make his IPL debut for DC as he is part of the lineup to face off against CSK at the Chepauk on Saturday, April 11.

LSG assistant coach Lance Klusener credits bowling for good IPL start Lucknow Super Giants assistant coach Lance Klusener believes the team's overseas batters are "sleeping giants" who will peak at the right time, while crediting the bowling unit for driving their recent wins. Strong contributions from the Indian batting and bowling core have kept LSG competitive, even as their overseas stars are yet to hit top gear.

Watch: Athletics Federation under fire after man walks onto track during race A man walking onto the track during a junior 200m race in New Delhi has sparked outrage, with fans slamming the Athletics Federation of India over safety lapses and poor event management.

CSK vs DC Live: Samson slams maiden ton for CSK, Mhatre retired out Chennai Super Kings (CSK) vs Delhi Capitals (DC) Live Score, IPL 2026: CSK (184/2 in 18 ovs) CSK take full control as Samson nears a century and Mhatre supports, with DC struggling for answers as the scoring surges and a big total looks firmly on the cards.

Catch of IPL 2026? Jansen pulls off a one-handed screamer to dismiss Ishan Kishan Marco Jansen pulled off a sensational one-handed catch at the boundary to dismiss Ishan Kishan in PBKS vs SRH, swinging the momentum firmly in Punjab's favour in Mullanpur.

Champion Rashid Khan doesn't need to reinvent to find form: GT assistant coachGT assistant coach Vijay Dahiya claimed that a champion bowler like Rashid Khan doesn't need to reinvent himself to regain his form. Rashid has shown signs of form during GT's win over DC in the IPL 2026.

Abhishek stuns PBKS with 28-ball 74 as SRH post third-highest powerplay total in IPLAbhishek Sharma lit up Mullanpur with a breathtaking display of power-hitting as Sunrisers Hyderabad stormed to the joint third-highest powerplay total in the history of the Indian Premier League on Saturday, April 11.

Generational talent: Sindhu reveals Ayush Shetty's winning formula in Asia ChampsAyush Shetty's breakthrough run at the Badminton Asia Championships has drawn praise from PV Sindhu, who sees something special in the youngster. After his win over the World No. 1, the spotlight is firmly on what comes next.

IPL 2026: Natarajan opens up on injury comeback, credits DC for strong supportT. Natarajan has credited Delhi Capitals' structured support system for helping him recover from a collarbone injury and regain form in IPL 2025. His improved fitness and yorker execution have made him a key figure in the franchise's bowling attack this season.

Zero points, no Dhoni: Can CSK fading fortress survive a DC raid?IPL 2026, CSK vs DC preview: A winless Chennai Super Kings take on a bruised Delhi Capitals at Chepauk on Saturday, desperate to snap a six-match home losing streak. Dewald Brevis is likely to return from injury, while CSK may have to wait longer for the comeback of their talisman, MS Dhoni.

Daryl Mitchell calls out Usman Tariq pause, R Ashwin praises New Zealand batterR Ashwin praised Daryl Mitchell for objecting to Usman Tariq's delivery pause during the PSL clash between Quetta Gladiators and Rawalpindi. He said batters are within their rights to step away if the pause is inconsistent and urged umpires and captains to address the issue.

Real Madrid title hopes over? Arbeloa fumes over poor referring in La LigaReal Madrid's draw against Girona, overshadowed by a contentious VAR call, has left their La Liga title hopes hanging by a thread. With pressure mounting over refereeing standards, Madrid must quickly regroup as they balance a fading domestic challenge with European ambitions.

PBKS vs SRH Highlights: Shreyas Iyer leads from front as Punjab hammer HyderabadPunjab (PBKS) vs SRH (Hyderabad) Highlights, IPL 2026: Shreyas Iyer masters another successful run-chase as Punjab make it three wins out of four games. The fifties from Priyansh Arya and Prabhsimran Singh set the platform for the hosts and their skipper finished it off. For Hyderabad, they will be left to rue their inability with the bat after a good start.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi thrilled as Virat Kohli gifts him signed cap after RCB heroicsIPL 2026, RR vs RCB: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi was over the moon after Virat Kohli gifted him a signed cap following his match-winning 78 against the Royal Challengers Bengaluru on Friday at the Barsapara Stadium in Guwahati.

How difficult it is to run a football club in India: The dos and don'tsBuilding a football club in India is less about profits and more about persistence in a system still finding its feet. Behind the romance of the game lies a risky, often unforgiving journey that tests belief, business sense, and patience.

IPL is more of a movie, PSL is actual cricket: Rilee Rossouw makes bizarre claimRilee Rossouw said the Indian Premier League is more entertainment-driven and influenced by Bollywood, making it feel like a movie, while the Pakistan Super League is more compact, intense, and focused on competitive cricket.

Ayush Shetty storms into Badminton Asia final, stuns World No.1 VitidsarnAyush Shetty stuns world No. 1 Kunlavut Vitidsarn to reach Badminton Asia final. The Indian fought back from a game down to win 10-21, 21-19, 21-17 in Ningbo.

From Virar to Churchgate: The daily grind that toughened up Ayush MhatreIPL 2026: Ayush Mhatre's start to IPL 2026 has been inconsistent, much like Chennai Super Kings' own transition phase. But for a player shaped by long train journeys and hard yards in Mumbai cricket, phases like these are not new.

SRH need 220-230 to give bowlers a chance to defend: Heinrich KlaasenHeinrich Klaasen believes that SunRisers Hyderabad bowling attack do not have the strength to defend less than 220-230 runs in the Indian Premier League. Klaasen has warned the batting unit, stating that they are leaving at least 40 runs in every single game.

Cooper Connolly inspired by Shaun Marsh aims to write his own story with PBKSIPL 2026: Cooper Connolly speaks about learning from Shaun Marsh and PBKS greats, focusing on his role as he looks to build his own path with Punjab Kings.

Hardik Pandya wins hearts with tribute to Rohit Sharma's 15-year MI journeyIPL 2026: Hardik Pandya paid a heartfelt tribute to Rohit Sharma on completing 15 years with Mumbai Indians, praising his leadership and lasting impact. Rohit joined MI in 2011 and played a key role in their five IPL title wins.

No second season blues: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi makes ex-India batter eat his own wordsIPL 2026, RR vs RCB: Robin Uthappa admitted he was happy to be proven wrong by Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, who scored a stunning 78 off 26 as Rajasthan Royals beat Royal Challengers Bengaluru on Friday in Guwahati.

How 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi is pushing RR batters to flirt with dangerIPL 2026: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's fearless hitting is not just winning matches for Rajasthan Royals, it is changing how his teammates approach their innings. As Dhruv Jurel put it, when the 15-year-old is batting, the game suddenly feels easier than it actually is.

Ayush Shetty vs Kunlavut Vitidsarn, Badminton Asia semis: Livestreaming detailsAyush Shetty stands one win away from a historic final as he faces World No. 1 Kunlavut Vitidsarn in Ningbo. Riding a giant-killing run, the young Indian now carries the nation's hopes on the continental stage.

Daryl Mitchell objects to Usman Tariq's pause, refuses to face Pak spinnerPSL 2026: A tactical standoff in Karachi saw Daryl Mitchell refuse to face Usman Tariq's controversial pause delivery, sparking a fresh debate on fair play. However, Tariq's Quetta Gladiators dismantled Rawalpindiz in a dominant 61-run victory on Friday.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
